oh wow
I was wondering where the names came from

Keter = crown (also conscious, also the force, the will, the nothingness-ish)
Binah = wisdom (or WIS, street smart kind of)
Hokmah = intelligent (INT stat in DnD)
Hesed/Chesed = mercy, compassion, love
Gevurah = discipline, judgement (STR stat maybe)
Tiferet = balance between elements
Netzah = endurance (can be interpreted as fortitute, or patience), kinda STA stat
Hod = humility (also the impermanence in some sense)
Yesod = form, the body, the foundation, CON stat
Malkuth = the living and the act of 'bringing heavenly life to earth' aka practicing all of the balance stuff of the whole other sefirah

Da'at = knowledge tho, in the between 6 other sefirah and more in the un-form side of the tree of life

ok the tldr of the concept is juliette promising that if castor goes through rituals and is super super faithful then he can take pollux's place and pollux won't have to suffer any more transformation rituals. castor who becomes the one fervently faithful, to the point of almost madness. castor who becomes cold and sharp and none of that softness that he had had as a child. castor who is the one judging those who are sinful, and who has long since lost any desire for freedom. pollux, who remembers the person his brother used to be, who mourns the quite but soft older brother that would read stories to him, now choked and buried beneath the mantle and weight of being a divus. pollux who still holds freedom tightly in his heart but knows that he'll never be able to reach for it, because how could he leave his brother?

Now that I've started reading Pandia's lore something made me curious
Is she like a succubus or something?
Or just a very deranged human being ?

She was a normal human who was a little bit of sadistic prison guard in her life, although she was left alone in that prison rotting for years until she died. She was always kinda freaky but the several years of isolation probably made her a lot worse. Eventually she (somehow, under rather vague conditions) got awakened as an awakener made of wax and Rogers sculpted her wax body so she could wander under the sun without melting. So she followed him in debt
Ooooooooh
Then what's her relationship with N about?
Was he there at that prison too?
I knew she says something about him not even looking at her during the riot , or something like that
And she also seems to not bother Sorel and act more decent around her
And if that isn't because of N then I should die this instant!
Or I could , as any normal person would , raise my relationship with her and read the remaining story instead of pestering others

We dont really know honestly, but it seems like N didnt findher interesting enough to make a deal with her. N was just there, got bored and seemingly left. She mentions a "hell cat" watching the prison but i dont know if thats a translation error or something i just dont understand. Regardless, N ignored her when they met and even forgot she existed.
Its implied that N promised to bail her out when the prison riot happened, but he didn't show up
Weird reaction then from Pandia to somehow respect Sorel , cause she seems like a pretty vengefull person
Wouldn't she have been more likely to act worse to Sorel , basically N's property , due to the fact that from her perspective he basically betrayed her?
Only sorel and keeper knows that she has a deal with N
Well I did thought that maybe she didn't know directly , but then the only reason she acts better towards Sorel might be because of N doing something
